modelIdentification.name: UCATT FOM (U-FOM) Extension Enumerations Module
modelIdentification.description: This module collects all enumerations (Enumerated datatypes) used in UCATT FOM modules.

reference.identification: RPR FOM v2.0 Base FOM Module and subsequent FOM Modules
SISO-STD-001.1-2015 Standard for Real-time Platform Reference Federation Object Model
SISO-STD-016-00-2016, Standard for UCATT Laser Engagement Interface

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Advanced_Platform_Damage_State_L1
enumeratedData.semantics: Level 1 of advanced platform damage state. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Platform Damage State, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 3.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Platform_Protective_Measures
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of passive/active platform protective measures in active use, i.e. enabled.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Lifeform_Protection_Measures
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of passive lifeform protective measures

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Advanced_Lifeform_Damage_State_L1
enumeratedData.semantics: Damage state level 1; category of advanced lifeform damage state. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Lifeform Damage state, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 2

enumeratedData.name: Enum_ALDS_Wounding_Area_L2
enumeratedData.semantics: Damagestate level 2, Type of advanced lifeform damage state wound areas. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Lifeform Damage state, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 2

enumeratedData.name: Enum_ALDS_Wounding_Type_L3
enumeratedData.semantics: Damagestate level 3, type of advanced lifeform damage state wound types. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Lifeform Damage state, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 2.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Wounding_TreatmentTier
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of treatment tier levels. The MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 4.11 list role 1-3 as the supported treatment tiers. However, for compatibilty and future extensions the enum list is extended.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Jamming_modes
enumeratedData.semantics: Simple EW enum

enumeratedData.name: Enum_MinefieldBreachType
enumeratedData.semantics: BreachedAll is used for non-defined mine types or area/lane-breach of combined minefields.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Obstacles
enumeratedData.semantics:
The type of obstacle; for blown out bridges, one may use craters insurmountable to e.g. all or only vehicles based on class. The anti LZ is used for EXCON defined area denial of air cavalry and/or paratroopers insertion etc.
Ed.note: For AntiLZ, the EXCON (centralized) or DO (decentralized) must use an algorithm to decide if the DO's are descending and landing into to the obstacle area, based on AGL or ASL, as an AntiLZ may be perfectly surmountable by anything already ground based!

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Obstacles_BreachForType
enumeratedData.semantics: The BreachFor types are sub seeding, so any obstacles breached for tracked vehicles will also allow any categories below, i.e. personnel, but not wheeled. According to MSG-140 Annex A, one can also use the breach modifier attribute instead of this.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Trajectory
enumeratedData.semantics: The fireline (e.g. by indirect fire/artillery shells) trajectory is used for EXCON visualization only.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_ValidFor
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of factions

enumeratedData.name: Enum_DecontaminationArea
enumeratedData.semantics: The types of decontamination capability.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_WeaponEffectModification
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of features that influence munition effectiveness.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_PrisonerHoldingArea
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of POW areas

enumeratedData.name: Enum_AirspaceType
enumeratedData.semantics: Type of ASM area

enumeratedData.name: Enum_RepairType
enumeratedData.semantics: DO repair types

enumeratedData.name: Enum_RestrictedType
enumeratedData.semantics: DO restricted for type

enumeratedData.name: Enum_RestrictedAreaType
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of restricted area

enumeratedData.name: Enum_NoFireArea
enumeratedData.semantics: Types of nofire restrictions

enumeratedData.name: Enum_UmpireCode
enumeratedData.semantics: Umpire Control-Gun codes. Refer to the SISO-STD-016-00-2016, Standard for UCATT Laser Engagement Interface for descriptions of the enum values

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Advanced_Infrastructure_Damage_State_L1
enumeratedData.semantics: Level 1 of advanced infrastructure damage state. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Infrastructure Damage state, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 5.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Obstacles_Breachtype
enumeratedData.semantics: These ENUMS are used for a visual/textual representation of breach areas. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 4.6.3

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Advanced_Platform_Damage_State_L2
enumeratedData.semantics: Level 2 of advanced platform damage state. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Platform Damage state, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 3. This enum is introduced for future use if level 2 damage state could be provided training area components.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Advanced_Platform_Damage_State_L3
enumeratedData.semantics: Level 3 of advanced platform damage state. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Platform Damage state, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 3. This enum is introduced for future use if level 3 damage state could be provided training area components.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Advanced_Platform_Damage_State_L4
enumeratedData.semantics: Level 4 of advanced platform damage state. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Platform Damage state, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 3. This enum is introduced for future use if level 4 damage state could be provided training area components.

enumeratedData.name: Enum_Advanced_Infrastructure_Damage_State_L2
enumeratedData.semantics: Level 2 of advanced infrastructure damage state. These ENUMS are part of the Advance Infrastructure Damage state, to support sublevel damage information. Ref MSG-140 Report Annex A, chapter 3, DO Status, table 5. This enum is introduced for future use if level 2 damage state could be provided training area components.

note.label: UCATTnoteEnumerations3
note.semantics: National unique for the German Army. Not to be used.
